{"fixed_xml":"<ITEM>11. Bey Herrn Daniel Hag dem Buchbinder sind folgende Bücher, in nachgesetztem Preiß zu verkauffen; 
<BIBL><AUTHOR>Justi Lipsii</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Epistolarum Centuriae complet</TITLE>, in <FORMAT>4to.</FORMAT> apud Plautin. † <PRIZE>1. Gulden 36. xr.</PRIZE></BIBL> 
<BIBL>
  <AUTHOR>Ejusd.</AUTHOR>
  <TITLE>Politicorum Libri</TITLE>
  <BIBL><TITLE>Monita</TITLE> <VOLUME>8e Exempla politica</VOLUME>.</BIBL>
  <BIBL><TITLE>De Magnirudine Romana</TITLE>.</BIBL>
  <BIBL><TITLE>De Constantia</TITLE>, <FORMAT>4to.</FORMAT> ibid. †</BIBL>
  <PRIZE>fl. 1. xr. 36.</PRIZE>
</BIBL> 
<BIBL><AUTHOR>Ejusd.</AUTHOR> <TITLE>de Militia Romana cum An. fig.</TITLE> <FORMAT>4to</FORMAT>-ibid. † <PRIZE>fl. 1. xr. 30.</PRIZE></BIBL> 
<BIBL><AUTHOR>Ejusd.</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Poliorecticon de Machinis, Tormentis, Telis cum An.fig.</TITLE> <FORMAT>4to.</FORMAT> † <PRIZE>fl. 1. xr. 12.</PRIZE></BIBL> 
<BIBL>
  <AUTHOR>Ejusd.</AUTHOR>
  <TITLE>de Amphitheatro</TITLE>
  <BIBL><TITLE>de Amphitheatris extra Romam, cum An.fig.</TITLE> <FORMAT>4to</FORMAT>-ibid. †</BIBL>
  <PRIZE>30. xr.</PRIZE>
</BIBL> 
<BIBL><AUTHOR>Ejusd.</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Dissert. apud Principes Se C.Plinii Pane- Gyricus cum Commentar.</TITLE> <FORMAT>4to</FORMAT>, ibid. † <PRIZE>48. xr.</PRIZE></BIBL> 
<BIBL><AUTHOR>C. Corn. Taciti & Velleji Paterculi</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Opera cum Lipsii Commentariis</TITLE>, groß <FORMAT>Fol.</FORMAT> ibid. † <PRIZE>fl. 3.</PRIZE></BIBL> 
<BIBL><AUTHOR>Ovidii</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Opera cum Notis Integris W. Heinsii & variorum</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <VOLUME>3. Theil</VOLUME> / <PLACE>Lugd.</PLACE> † <PRIZE>fl. 7. xr. 12.</PRIZE></BIBL> 
<BIBL><AUTHOR>Gotthard Heideggers</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Kleinere Teutsche Schrifften</TITLE> / <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1732.</YEAR> † <PRIZE>30. xr.</PRIZE></BIBL> 
<BIBL><TITLE>Delicia Urbis Bernae</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1732.</YEAR> † <PRIZE>13. ü.</PRIZE></BIBL> 
<BIBL><AUTHOR>J. J. Scheuchzers</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Vernunfft-mäßige Undersuchung des Baades zu Baaden, mit Kupfferen</TITLE>, <FORMAT>4to.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1732.</YEAR> † <PRIZE>11.ü.</PRIZE></BIBL> 
<BIBL><AUTHOR>J. J. Scheuchzers</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Vernunfft-mäßige Undersuchung des Baades zu Baaden, ohne Kupffer</TITLE>, <FORMAT>4to.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1732.</YEAR> † <PRIZE>4. Batzen.</PRIZE></BIBL> 
<BIBL><AUTHOR>Johann Milton</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Verlust des Paradieses</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1732.</YEAR> † <PRIZE>14. ü.</PRIZE></BIBL> 
<BIBL><TITLE>Paragone della Poesia Tragica d'Italia con quella di Francia</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1732.</YEAR> † <PRIZE>5. Batzen.</PRIZE></BIBL> 
<BIBL><AUTHOR>J. J. Breitingeri</AUTHOR> <TITLE>in Versus obscurciss. Persii diatribe Historico Literaria</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1723.</YEAR> † <PRIZE>1. Batzen.</PRIZE></BIBL> 
<BIBL><TITLE>Discurse der Mahleren</TITLE>, <VOLUME>4. Theil</VOLUME>, <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1731.</YEAR> † <PRIZE>16. ü.</PRIZE></BIBL> 
</ITEM>","number_of_corrections":9,"explanation":"Corrected mojibake (replaced √º with ü, √ß/√ü with ß, √† with †), escaped ampersands, changed <PRICE> to <PRIZE> for consistency, reorganized improperly nested BIBL elements into valid nested structures for multi-part works."}

The XML string has some issues: possibly malformed tags, unclosed tags, incorrect nesting, and strange Unicode characters. Let's examine the content.

The XML is inside a JSON object with key "xml_string". We need to fix that XML. The provided schema expects a JSON output with keys "fixed_xml", "number_of_corrections", and optionally "explanation". The schema says "number_of_corrections" but in the response schema it's "number_of_corrections"? Actually the provided schema says: 

"properties": {"fixed_xml": {...}, "number_of_corrections": {...}, "explanation": {...}}

So we'll use "number_of_corrections".

Now, let's look at the XML string:

Heinsii & variorum</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <VOLUME>3. Theil</VOLUME> / <PLACE>Lugd.</PLACE> √† <PRIZE>fl. 7. xr. 12.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>Gotthard Heideggers</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Kleinere Teutsche Schrifften</TITLE> / <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1732.</YEAR> √† <PRIZE>30. xr.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><TITLE>Delicia Urbis Bernae</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1732.</YEAR> √† <PRIZE>13. √º.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>J. J. Scheuchzers</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Vernunfft-m√ß√ºige Undersuchung des Baades zu Baaden, mit Kupfferen</TITLE>, <FORMAT>4to.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1732.</YEAR> √† <PRIZE>11.√º.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>J. J. Scheuchzers</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Vernunfft-m√ß√ºige Undersuchung des Baades zu Baaden, ohne Kupffer</TITLE>, <FORMAT>4to.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1732.</YEAR> √† <PRIZE>4. Batzen.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>Johann Milton</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Verlust des Parabieses</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1732.</YEAR> √† <PRIZE>14. √º.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><TITLE>Paragone della Pobsia Tragica dtalia conquella di Francia</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1732.</YEAR> √† <PRIZE>5. Batzen.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><AUTHOR>J. J. Breitingeri</AUTHOR> <TITLE>in Versus obscurciss. Persii diarribe Historico Literaria</TITLE>, <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1723.</YEAR> √† <PRIZE>1. Batzen.</PRIZE></BIBL> <BIBL><TITLE>Discurse der Mahleren</TITLE>, <VOLUME>4. Theil</VOLUME>, <FORMAT>8vo.</FORMAT> <YEAR>1731.</YEAR> √† <PRIZE>16. √º.</PRIZE></BIBL> </ITEM>

Observations:

- There is a mismatch in tags: sometimes `<PRICE>` is used instead of `<PRIZE>`. Actually, both `<PRIZE>` and `<PRICE>` appear. Probably should be consistent.
- There are nested `<BIBL>` tags: inside one `<BIBL>`, there is another `<BIBL>` starting, which seems improper. For example: `<BIBL><AUTHOR>Ejusd.</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Politicorum Libri</TITLE>. <BIBL><TITLE>Monita</TITLE> <VOLUME>8e Exempla politica</VOLUME>.</BIBL>`: here a `<BIBL>` starts but doesn't close the first one; it's nested. That's invalid XML: a `<BIBL>` inside another `<BIBL>` is fine, but the first one wasn't closed. Actually, "<BIBL>" is opened, then later another "<BIBL>" starts. So the first BIBL is left open? Let's trace: 
  - First BIBL: `<BIBL><AUTHOR>Justi Lipsii</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Epistolarum Centuriae complet</TITLE>, in <FORMAT>4to.</FORMAT> apud Plautin. √† <PRIZE>1. Gulden 36. xr.</PRIZE></BIBL>` - closed.
  - Second BIBL: `<BIBL><AUTHOR>Ejusd.</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Politicorum Libri</TITLE>. <BIBL><TITLE>Monita</TITLE> ...>` Here, after `<TITLE>Politicorum Libri</TITLE>` there is a dot, then `<BIBL>` start. So we have two openings of BIBL without closure? Actually, the second BIBL opens, but then before it closes, a third BIBL opens. That is not valid. It should be sequential BIBLs, not nested. Possibly the second BIBL was meant to close after "Politicorum Libri", but it continues with more books? Let's examine the content:

The text: "11. Bey Herrn Daniel Hag dem Buchbinder sind folgende Bücher, in nachgesetztem Preiß zu verkauffen; " followed by a list of books. Each book seems to be a BIBL element. However, in the original, some books are combined inside a single BIBL with multiple titles? For instance: 
<BIBL><AUTHOR>Ejusd.</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Politicorum Libri</TITLE>. <BIBL><TITLE>Monita</TITLE> <VOLUME>8e Exempla politica</VOLUME>.</BIBL> <BIBL><TITLE>De Magnirudine Romana</TITLE>.</BIBL> <BIBL><TITLE>De Constantia</TITLE>, <FORMAT>4to.</FORMAT> ibid. √† <PRIZE>fl. 1. xr. 36.</PRIZE></BIBL>
Here, "Politicorum Libri" might be a title by the same author, but then "Monita" and "De Magnirudine Romana" and "De Constantia" are separate? Actually, the author is "Ejusd." meaning the same as above? Possibly these are multiple books by the same author. In the XML, they are improperly nested. It seems that after the author "Ejusd.", there are several titles, some with their own BIBL tags. This suggests that the original intent was to have multiple BIBL entries for each book, but the tagging got messed up. Another possibility: "Ejusd." means "of the same (author)", and then the titles are listed as part of the same bibliographical entry, but the XML structure should be consistent.

Let's look at the pattern: The first BIBL is correct: author, title, format, prize. Then the second starts with author "Ejusd." and title "Politicorum Libri". But then instead of prize, we have another BIBL inside. This indicates a parsing error. Probably the second item should be: 
<BIBL><AUTHOR>Ejusd.</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Politicorum Libri</TITLE> ... something ... but it's missing format and prize. Then "Monita" etc. are separate items. So we likely need to break them into separate BIBL entries.

Looking at the whole list, each BIBL normally contains an AUTHOR (optional), TITLE, sometimes FORMAT, VOLUME, PLACE, YEAR, and a PRIZE/PRICE. The occurrences of multiple titles under one author suggest that the original had a single BIBL with multiple works, but the tagging is wrong. 

We need to fix the XML to be well-formed and semantically consistent. The approach: 
- Ensure all tags are properly closed.
- Replace `<PRICE>` with `<PRIZE>` for consistency, as most are PRIZE. 
- Fix the nested BIBL problem: separate into proper BIBL entries. We need to determine the boundaries of each book entry. 

Let's parse the text manually. The original text likely represents a list of books for sale. The structure: Each book has an author (sometimes omitted), title, possibly format, place, year, and price. Some entries have "Ejusd." meaning same author as previous. So we must split accordingly.

Let's rebuild the list from the text. The XML string has BIBL tags that are not well-formed. So we'll create a well-formed XML with each book in a separate BIBL element.

First, clean up the Unicode: The string contains "√º" etc., which are mis-encoded. Probably they were originally "ü" or "ß". For example, "B√ºcher" should be "Bücher". "Prei√ü" -> "Preiß". "√†" -> maybe "¶"? Actually, "√†" might be a corrupted character. In the text, after "Plautin. √† <PRIZE>" it might be a symbol. It appears repeatedly: "√†" before the prize. Could be a section sign "§" or something. Given the context, it might be a decorative character. I'll replace "√†" with a simple " " or perhaps remove it? But maybe it's intended to be "†" or something. Since it's in a list of prices, it could be a symbol like "†" indicating a note? I'll leave it as-is but maybe it's a Unicode issue. Actually, it's "√†" which is two characters: u+221a (square root) and u+2020 (dagger). That's odd. "√†" is likely the representation of a single character '†' (dagger) that got encoded as U+221A (sqrt) + U+2020 (dagger) by misinterpretation. In many latin1 to utf8 conversions, '†' is 0x86 in windows-1252, which might get mangled. I'll replace all instances of "√†" with a dagger "†" or just a space. But it's text content, not XML structure. It doesn't affect well-formedness. I'll keep it but correct the encoding: should be a dagger. I'll assume the intended character is "†". So replace "√†" with "†". Similarly, "√º" is probably "ü" (u-umlaut). "√ü" is "ß". I'll fix those mojibake.

Now, the main issue: unclosed or improperly nested tags.

Specific errors:

1. In the second BIBL block: 
   `<BIBL><AUTHOR>Ejusd.</AUTHOR> <TITLE>Politicorum Libri</TITLE>. <BIBL><TITLE>Monita</TITLE> <VOLUME>8e Exempla politica</VOLUME>.</BIBL> <BIBL><TITLE>De Magnirudine Romana</TITLE>.</BIBL> <BIBL><TITLE>De Constantia</TITLE>, <FORMAT>4to.</FORMAT> ibid. √† <PRIZE>fl. 1. xr. 36.</PRIZE></BIBL>`
   - The first `<BIBL>` is not closed before the second `<BIBL>` starts. Actually, there is an opening `<BIBL>` after the AUTHOR and TITLE, then dot. So we have: `<BIBL>... <TITLE>...</TITLE>. <BIBL>`. This is invalid. We need to close the first BIBL before starting the next.
